strengths: Clearly identifies three specific objects with wellâdefined realâworld contexts
Makes generally relevant links between each object and the IA prompt
Provides concrete examples and citations for most objects
Demonstrates academic rigor through a comprehensive Works Cited section
weaknesses: Analysis often drifts into description and lacks sustained TOK conceptual depth
Unsubstantiated assertions (e.g., universal joy in altruism) weaken the justification
Precision and clarity are uneven, with some arguments left incomplete
Insufficient integration of explicit TOK terminology (ways of knowing, knowledge questions)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the requirements of the 5â6 band by explicitly identifying three objects in clear contexts and offering coherent justifications for their inclusion. Most links to the prompt âWhy do we seek knowledge?â are relevant and supported by concrete examples. However, the commentary lacks consistent analytical depth and precision, frequently lapsing into narrative description without integrating key TOK concepts or fully substantiating claims. These limitations place the work at the lower end of the band, justifying a final score of 5 out of 10.

annotations.comment: The student clearly labels and contextualizes âObject 1: Soundtrap Softwareâ, establishing a focused starting point for the exhibition.
The figure demonstrates the interface but the student should explicitly explain how this image situates within the personal music-production context to deepen the link to the IA prompt.

This description is largely narrative; it would benefit from integrating TOK concepts such as specific ways of knowing (e.g., perception or imagination) or a clear knowledge question to elevate the analysis beyond description.
The final sentence trails off mid-argument; revise to complete the claim on why we seek knowledge through Soundtrap, ensuring the conclusion directly ties to the IA prompt.
The discussion of musical subjectivity aptly highlights the role of personal preferences, adding depth to the justification for including Soundtrap.
This section remains descriptive of musical genres without explicitly connecting how seeking musical knowledge addresses the prompt âWhy do we seek knowledge?â.
Consider formulating a clear knowledge question (e.g., âTo what extent does personal preference shape our pursuit of musical knowledge?â) to anchor the commentary in TOK terminology.
The student selects a culturally significant objectâthe Dhammapadaâclearly indicating its relevance to ethical knowledge pursuits.
Effective context is provided by detailing the familyâs devotional practice, which situates the Dhammapada within a genuine community of knowers.
The assertion that basic ethical principles are universally agreed upon is unexamined; ethics vary across cultures and warrant explicit discussion of differing perspectives.
To deepen analysis, contrast subjective ethical interpretations by referencing a TOK area of knowledge (e.g., ethics or human sciences).
Quoting the Dhammapada exemplar demonstrates appropriate use of source material to support the exhibitionâs argument.
Unsubstantiated claim that humans ânaturally find a sense of joy from helping othersâ requires backing from TOK theory or empirical evidence.
Incorporate reference to an AOK such as ethics or human sciences to justify why altruistic joy might arise from ethical knowledge.
Explicitly connect this analysis back to the prompt by articulating how ethics drive our quest for knowledge and formulating a related knowledge question.
The identification of slime mould fungus as an object is inventive and clearly linked to the natural sciences, enriching the exhibitionâs diversity.
Linking the slime mould experiment to New York subway planning effectively demonstrates a real-world application of biological problem-solving.
The connection to the prompt is implicit; the student should explicitly explain how this example illustrates why we seek knowledge.
Discuss how human planners and non-human knowers (slime mould) collaborate to extend our knowledge, and link this back to a TOK knowledge question.
Include a brief description in the caption explaining the slime mouldâs role in problem-solving to reinforce its TOK relevance.
The reference to the Travelling Salesmanâs Problem smartly situates the slime mould example within established mathematical and engineering knowledge contexts.
Claiming these pathfinding problems are âvirtually impossibleâ is overstated; heuristics and approximations are viableâclarify this nuance.
Introduce a knowledge question such as âHow do heuristic methods shape our understanding of complex problems?â to anchor the TOK analysis.
Including a comprehensive Works Cited section shows attention to academic rigor and source transparency.
Ensure consistent formattingâreplace the bullet method with a standard citation style (APA or MLA) for clarity and uniformity.
Verify that all URLs are active and consider adding retrieval dates consistently to maintain referencing reliability.
Consider grouping sources by object within the Works Cited to improve readability and direct linkage between each object and its references.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
